hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — 5, Queen, 10. You bet. Your opponent called, staying patient.
Turn — King. You bet. Your opponent raised — they just connected.
River — 8. Your opponent bet again when checked to.

wrongReasons: 3 Q has similar strength, but look at when the aggression shifted — the turn card connects with the correct hand in a way it doesn't connect with 3 Q.
J 9 makes a straight flush here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
4 J doesn't benefit from the turn card the way the correct hand does. A player holding only high card wouldn't have the confidence to raise on that street.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K J

Your opponent called the flop with overcards, then the turn card gave them a pair. The raise on the turn — right after that card landed — is the tell: passive until they hit, then immediately aggressive.
